channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Newcastle not looking to make Howe change at moment - CEO
item.description: Newcastle United CEO David Hopkinson says he does not have a stance on the future of head coach Eddie Howe, but the club are "not looking to make a change at the moment".

item.title: Arsenal's 11 international withdrawals - injury crisis or crafty caution?
item.description: With 11 Arsenal players withdrawing from international duty, are the Gunners facing an injury crisis or are they simply being cautious?

item.title: Ranking the 10 most exciting wingers in the world
item.description: Few things in football are more exciting than an in-form wide man, but who are the most exciting in the world right night?

item.title: Why innovative Canadian league is trialling daylight offside rule
item.description: BBC Sport looks at why the Canadian Premier League is trialling Arsene Wenger's 'daylight' offside rule in 2026.

item.title: Ghana sack manager Addo, 72 days before World Cup
item.description: Ghana dismiss manager Otto Addo, 72 days before the start of the 2026 World Cup, following a run of defeats.

item.title: Kosovo in euphoria as they dream of World Cup history
item.description: Having gained independence in 2008, Kosovo are one game away from qualifying for the World Cup and sparking "the biggest celebrations ever seen in Pristina".

item.title: How Scotland found their base camp for 'travelling' World Cup
item.description: Gary Dicker and Steven Naismith explain why Scotland picked Charlotte FC as their World Cup base.

item.title: De Zerbi in advanced talks to become Spurs manager
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur enter into advanced talks with Roberto de Zerbi, with the former Brighton manager now willing to take charge immediately, according to sources.

item.title: Who are the Japan players England should look out for?
item.description: England face Japan in their next friendly match as they continue their preparations for the World Cup this summer. BBC Sport takes a look at the Japanese players to look out for.

item.title: How Sky can ignore limit on its TV picks & why fans are fed up
item.description: Premier League games being moved to Friday or Monday are a headache for the fans of some clubs. Is the system fair, and how does it work for Sky?

item.title: Wales v Northern Ireland - the match nobody wants
item.description: Wales host Northern Ireland in a friendly in Cardiff on Tuesday because both teams lost their World Cup play-off semi-finals on Thursday.

item.title: Fernandez on Real Madrid shortlist - Tuesday's gossip
item.description: Chelsea midfielder Enzo Fernandez is on Real Madrid's shortlist, Juventus are eyeing Manchester United's Manuel Ugarte, Newcastle receive a boost in their bid to keep hold of Sandro Tonali, plus more.

item.title: Five Euro 2028 tickets 'cost same as World Cup parking space'
item.description: Uefa is to expected to broadly freeze prices at Euro 2028 - meaning supporters would be able to buy five tickets for the cost of one parking space at a World Cup stadium this summer.

item.title: 'This is bigger than football' - DR Congo bid to end 52-year World Cup wait
item.description: DR Congo face Jamaica in a World Cup play-off, hoping to reach the tournament for the first time since 1974, a campaign memorable for the wrong reasons.

item.title: World Cup risks being 'stage for repression' - Amnesty
item.description: The 2026 World Cup risks becoming "a stage for repression and a platform for authoritarian practices", according to a new report from human rights campaign group Amnesty.

item.title: Fit-again McGinn was 'petrified' he would miss World Cup
item.description: John McGinn has been a Scotland international for a decade now as he looks forward to the World Cup after a big injury scare.

item.title: Tuchel not angry over England withdrawals
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel says he is not disappointed with the players who have pulled out of the squad for Tuesday's friendly against Japan.

item.title: Wales boss Bellamy to keep 'best job in the world'
item.description: Craig Bellamy underlines his commitment to staying on as Wales head coach, describing it as the best job in the world.

item.title: Cardiff City damages claim over Emiliano Sala death dismissed
item.description: The club, which was claiming £104m in compensation, will now have to fork out about £400,000.

item.title: 'That's world class' - pundits analyse Russo's hat-trick vs Tottenham
item.description: BBC pundits Fara Williams and Anita Asante analyse Alessia Russo's brilliant hat‑trick against Tottenham, discussing whether her best position is the number nine or 10 role for Arsenal.

item.title: Tudor's five Premier League games as Spurs boss
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look back at Igor Tudor's five Premier League games in charge as Tottenham Hotspur boss.

item.title: Chelsea leave it late to win seven-goal thriller over Villa
item.description: Chelsea secure a vital three points as they beat Aston Villa 4-3 to go second in the league table and get back on track for Champions League qualification.

item.title: Brighton condemn Leicester City to seventh-straight league loss
item.description: A lone goal from Brighton's Kiko Seike is enough to condemn Leicester City to a seventh-straight loss in the WSL while Albion move up to sixth.

item.title: Siren scores screamer as West Ham draw at London City
item.description: Oona Siren's stunning volley helps Women's Super League relegation battlers West Ham fight back to earn a point against London City Lionesses.

item.title: Former West Brom keeper Godden dies aged 70
item.description: Popular former West Bromwich Albion goalkeeper Tony Godden dies at the age of 70.

item.title: Shades of Gascoigne - the rise of England's Anderson
item.description: Four years ago Elliot Anderson was helping Bristol Rovers to League Two promotion - this summer he could be a key player for England at the World Cup. BBC Sport charts his rise.

item.title: Two teams, three games, six days - O'Neill's unique predicament
item.description: BBC Sport NI looks at how Northern Ireland and Blackburn Rovers manager Michael O'Neill will navigate a unique six-day stretch that will include games against Wales, Birmingham City and a relegation six-pointer against West Brom.

item.title: Hodgson happy being retired but Bristol City hard to turn down
item.description: Roy Hodgson says there is a lot he misses about football after coming out of retirement to become caretaker manager of Bristol City.

item.title: I designed Everton's new stadium - now I have an 1878 tattoo
item.description: Architect Dan Meis talks about the challenges of designing Everton's Hill Dickinson Stadium - including relegation battles, new owners, a pandemic and global war.

item.title: Watch: Brown's last Ayr game as Championship leaders also stumble
item.description: Watch our round-up of highlights from Scotland's lower leagues as Scott Brown's Ayr United reign ends in defeat, Championship leaders St Johnstone drop home points, Stenhousemuir miss a chance to top the third tier, while Spartans and East Kilbride draw at the top of League 2.

item.title: Scotland's World Cup build-up begins with narrow Japan loss
item.description: Watch the best of the action from Hampden as Junya Ito's 84th-minute strike consigns Scotland to defeat in their first World Cup warm-up friendly.

item.title: Russo scores hat-trick as Arsenal beat Spurs
item.description: Striker Alessia Russo nets a first-half hat-trick as Arsenal beat north London rivals Tottenham 5-2 in front of a crowd of more than 46,000 fans at Emirates Stadium.

item.title: Man City edge closer to WSL title by beating Man Utd
item.description: Vivianne Miedema's double helps Manchester City edge closer to the Women's Super League title with a comfortable derby win over neighbours United.

item.title: 10-player Liverpool survive fightback to win Merseyside derby
item.description: Ten-player Liverpool hold on for their first Women's Super League away win of the season with a hard-fought victory over local rivals Everton at Goodison Park.

item.title: 75% of fans oppose VAR in Premier League, says FSA
item.description: Fans believe VAR is ruining goal celebrations and has not improved football since its introduction, according to a Football Supporters' Association survey.

item.title: BBC Sport experts choose England World Cup XIs - who would you pick?
item.description: With one game to go until Thomas Tuchel picks his England World Cup squad, BBC Sport reporters choose their starting XIs for the summer. Who would you pick?

item.title: Celtic legend Brown leaves Ayr by mutual consent
item.description: Former Celtic and Scotland captain Scott Brown stands down as Ayr United manager "by mutual consent".

item.title: How to follow Ivory Coast v Scotland on BBC TV, radio & online
item.description: The details of the BBC's television, radio and digital coverage of Tuesday's friendly between Scotland and the Ivory Coast.

item.title: Celtic pay out five-figure sum to sexual abuse victim
item.description: The survivor raised a legal action after claiming he was attacked by Frank Cairney at Celtic Boys Club in 1989.

item.title: 'We've had closure' - O'Shea ready to park Czech loss
item.description: Republic of Ireland defender Dara O'Shea says the squad have had "closure" from their penalty shootout defeat to Czech Republic on Thursday which ended their World Cup dream.

item.title: Salah set completely new standards - Klopp
item.description: Jurgen Klopp says Mohamed Salah is an "all-time great" at Liverpool and that he "would not be surprised if he plays another six or seven years" after the forward announced he will leave the club at the end of the season.

item.title: Wales' World Cup dream ends in pain of penalty shootout
item.description: Wales' hopes of reaching the 2026 World Cup are ended by Bosnia-Herzegovina in Cardiff as the away side score a late equaliser before winning on penalties in their play-off semi-final.

item.title: Bellamy bewildered by Wales' shootout defeat
item.description: Head coach Craig Bellamy says Wales did all they could to prepare for penalties after their World Cup qualification bid ended with shootout defeat to Bosnia-Herzegovina.

item.title: The 'daredevil child' scouted by an ex-England captain
item.description: The origin story of Manchester United and England's Jess Park, from her grassroots in Brough to being scouted by Steph Houghton.

item.title: O'Neill positive about Northern Ireland future despite Italy defeat
item.description: Northern Ireland manager Michael O'Neill insists the future is bright for his side after they lose their World Cup play-off semi-final against Italy, as they're beaten 2-0 in Bergamo.

item.title: 'Stunning, breathtaking, glorious!' James gives Wales lead against Bosnia
item.description: Dan James scores a stunning long-range effort to give Wales a crucial lead in their World Cup Play-off semi-final against Bosnia-Herzegovina in Cardiff.

item.title: Tonali strike puts Italy ahead against Northern Ireland
item.description: Watch as Newcastle United midfielder Sandro Tonali gives Italy the lead against Northern Ireland in their World Cup play-off semi-final in Bergamo.

item.title: Hume rues missed chances as Northern Ireland lose to Italy
item.description: Northern Ireland captain Trai Hume speaks to BBC Sport NI's Gavin Andrews after his side lose their play-off semi-final 2-0 to Italy in Bergamo, bringing their hopes of reaching the 2026 World Cup to an end.

item.title: Watch Celtic's McAneny's world record-breaking halfway line goal
item.description: Celtic's Maria McAneny's goal straight from kick-off against Hibernian in December has been confirmed as the fastest in the history of women's football.

item.title: Man Utd come from behind twice but lose first leg to Bayern
item.description: Manchester United fan Pernille Harder scored twice against her childhood club at Old Trafford as Bayern Munich gained a precious first-leg advantage in their Champions League quarter-final tie.

item.title: Barcelona put six past Madrid in quarter-final first leg
item.description: Barcelona took control of their Women's Champions League quarter-final with an impressive, dominant away first-leg victory at Spanish rivals Real Madrid.

item.title: Madrid's Caicedo scores 'wonder strike' against Barcelona
item.description: Real Madrid's Linda Caicedo scores with a stunning effort in her team's 6-2 defeat to Barcelona in the first leg of their women's Champions League quarter-final.

item.title: Premier League quiz: Name every manager departure this season
item.description: A Premier League club has parted ways with a permanent head coach on nine occasions this season - can you name them all?

item.title: How do Man City replace captain Silva if he leaves?
item.description: Manchester City could find themselves without the services of their manager and captain next season with the futures of both Pep Guardiola and Bernardo Silva in question beyond this summer.

item.title: What happened to former Chelsea wonderkid Musonda?
item.description: Charly Musonda could have been a household name but his story is one of frustration and unfulfilled potential.

item.title: Managing in an international break - and what to do when your star player does not come home
item.description: Tony Pulis explains why international breaks can be an opportunity as well as a problem for managers, and what he did when one of his players did not return on time.

item.title: The remarkable return of Roy Hodgson aged 78
item.description: Roy Hodgson's idea of celebrating 50 years in management is not to sit back reflecting on his career, but instead to return to the dugout at Bristol City.

item.title: 'Like being stabbed in stomach' - Everton's Snoeijs on endometriosis
item.description: Everton forward Katja Snoeijs explains her journey to getting a diagnosis of endometriosis.

item.title: All February and March's done deals, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: How does Tudor's record compare to other Tottenham bosses?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team compare Igor Tudor's managerial record to other Tottenham managers of recent years.

item.title: Why are Scotland playing Ivory Coast in Liverpool?
item.description: Scotland continue their World Cup preparations with a friendly against Ivory Coast on Tuesday, but why is this away game being played at Everton's new stadium in Liverpool?

item.title: Who is one card away from suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: When can Arsenal win the Premier League?
item.description: Arsenal are nine points clear at the top of the Premier League, but when can they officially win the title?

item.title: What happens when a manager gets suspended?
item.description: Ever wondered what a touchline ban entails? BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain what punishment managers actually face.

item.title: Which club has the most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the record for most red cards in a Premier League season

item.title: Rangers' Matondo set for Norway move - gossip
item.description: Rangers winger and Celtic midfielder on the move as Gordon Strachan backs Aberdeen appointment.

item.title: Does McLeish keep his job if he keeps St Mirren up?
item.description: Craig McLeish might be a surprise choice to lead St Mirren in a fight against relegation but the interim manager has plans to stick around.

item.title: Lang's surgery for thumb injury 'went well'
item.description: Galatasaray midfielder Noa Lang is set to have surgery in Liverpool after suffering a "serious cut" at Anfield.

item.title: Who are the record Champions League goalscorers?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team looks at which players have scored the most goals in the Champions League.

item.title: Why ex-Palace winger Olise is now in Ballon d'Or contention at Bayern
item.description: Michael Olise has been in superb form for Bayern Munich this season, has the French winger put himself in contention for the Ballon d'Or?

item.title: Laporta re-elected Barcelona president for fourth term
item.description: Joan Laporta vows Barcelona will be "unstoppable" as he is re-elected club president for a further five years.

item.title: From Bodo to Brentford - ranking this season's biggest overachievers
item.description: Bodo/Glimt must be the most romantic story in European football in decades but are they the team that has defied the odds the most this season? Here are all 10 contenders...

item.title: Why Real Madrid have 'complete confidence' in teenager Pitarch
item.description: When an 18-year-old makes Real Madrid history in a crucial Champions League tie against Manchester City, it inevitably draws acclaim and attention. BBC Sport takes a look at rising star Thiago Pitarch.

item.title: Bohs' Brennan earns first Republic of Ireland call-up
item.description: Teenage Bohemians midfielder Aoibhe Brennan earns her first call-up to the Republic of Ireland senior squad for their World Cup 2027 qualifying double-header against Poland in April.

item.title: England's Kearns reveals she has had a miscarriage
item.description: England international Missy Bo Kearns says "the past weeks have been filled with a kind of sadness that's hard to explain" as she reveals she has had a miscarriage.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
